title: Macrochloa arenaria
intro: Macrochloa arenaria, also known as desert grass or desert beard grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Macrochloa arenaria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Macrochloa arenaria include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
